# Ragmir

Ragmir is an open-source local-first RAG library, CLI, and MCP server. It indexes confidential
documents locally and returns cited retrieval context to AI agents.

## Core facts

- Published as `@jcode.labs/ragmir` on npm.
- Stores its local index with LanceDB and is offline by default.
- Returns cited passages, not LLM-generated answers.
- Supports text, code, Markdown, PDF, common Office/OpenDocument formats, EPUB, HTML, CSV, JSON,
  and YAML. Scanned images need opt-in OCR.

## Use it when

- A confidential corpus is too large for an agent context window.
- Agents need source citations from PDFs, specifications, or technical records.
- The index and documents must stay on the local machine.

For code navigation or a tiny corpus, use native code search or pass the text directly instead.

## Quick start

```sh
pnpm add -D @jcode.labs/ragmir
pnpm exec rgr setup
pnpm exec rgr sources add "docs/**/*.md"
pnpm exec rgr ingest
```

Use the generated MCP helper with Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, OpenCode, or Cline. See the
[repository README](https://github.com/jcode-works/jcode-ragmir#readme) for API and CLI examples.
